LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANDRE PATTERSON'S MOTION TO REINSTATE COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 431.10-431.70, 469-475, 585-587.5. NO APPEARANCES. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS NOT RIPE FOR REVIEW AS TO DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS ACTIVE AGAINST DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES; REINSTATEMENT IS NOT NECESSARY. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO REINSTATE HIS COMPLAINT AGAINST DEFENDANTS ANDREA MILANO AND LITTLER MENDELSON, THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS IMPROPER. ON MARCH 11, 2013, THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANTS ANDREA MILANO AND LITTLER MENDELSON'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. THE COMPLAINT CANNOT BE REINSTATED ON THE GROUNDS SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFF. FURTHERMORE, IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER PLAINTIFF SERVED THESE DEFENDANTS ANDREA MILANO'S AND LITTLER MENDELSON. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING MOTION. DISMISSAL IS NOT WARRANTED. DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES MOVE TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT BASED ON A COURT ORDER, FILED ON MARCH 11, 2013, WHERE THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANTS MILANO AND LITTLER MENDELSON, PC'S MOTION TO STRIKE SLAPPBACK COMPLAINT PER CCP 425.18. HOWEVER, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES WERE NOT THE MOVING PARTY ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE SLAPPBACK COMPLAINT. THE DISMISSAL OF THE COMPLAINT AGAINST DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES BASED ON THE COURT ORDER GRANTING MILANO AND LITTLER MENDELSON, PC'S MOTION IS NOT WARRANTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES ARGUE THE MERITS OF THEIR OWN ANTI-SLAPPBACK MOTION IN THE INSTANT MOTION TO DISMISS, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES DID NOT STATE THESE GROUNDS FOR RELIEF IN THEIR NOTICE OF MOTION. THE COURT CANNOT GRANT DIFFERENT RELIEF, OR RELIEF ON DIFFERENT GROUNDS, THAN STATED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. (LURI V. GREENWALD (2003) 107 CA4TH 1119, 1124, 132 CR2D 680, 684.) MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING WITHIN 10 DAYS TO STATE A PROPER GROUND. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO PROVIDE A PROPER GROUND FOR RELIEF IN THEIR NOTICE. (CRC 3.1110(A).) THE DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINTS FILED IN OTHER ACTIONS IS NOT A PROPER GROUND FOR RELIEF ON A DEMURRER. (CCP 430.10. SEE NOTICE, P. 1:22 - 2:20.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IN THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE WITHIN 10 DAYS TO STATE PROPER GROUNDS. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO PROVIDE A PROPER GROUND FOR RELIEF IN THEIR NOTICE. (CRC 3.1110(A).) THE DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINTS FILED IN OTHER ACTIONS IS NOT A PROPER GROUND TO STRIKE A PRAYER FOR RELIEF IN THE FORM OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (CCP 436. SEE NOTICE, P. 2:22 - 3:16.) THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE OTHER GROUNDS ASSERTED IN DEFENDANTS' POINTS AND AUTHORITIES THAT ARE NOT STATED IN THE NOTICE. (POINTS & AUTH., P. 10:22 - 11:15.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Mtn To Reinstate Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 431.10-431.70, 469-475, 585-587.5
Off Calendar Apr-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ANDRE PATTERSON's Motion To Reinstate Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 431.10-431.70, 469-475, 585-587.5 IS Denied. The requested relief is not ripe for review as to Defendants Catholic Charities and Marinella Goncalves. Plaintiff's Complaint is active against Defendants Catholic Charities and Marinella Goncalves; reinstatement is not necessary. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks to reinstate his Complaint against Defendants Andrea Milano and Littler Mendelson, the requested relief is improper. On March 11, 2013, the Court granted Defendants Andrea Milano and Littler Mendelson's Special Motion to Strike. The Complaint cannot be reinstated on the grounds sought by Plaintiff. Furthermore, it is unclear whether Plaintiff served these Defendants Andrea Milano's and Littler Mendelson. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc And Anti-Slappback Mtn For An Order Dismissing This Matter In Its Entirety/Alt Dismissing This Matter
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SEAN MORIARTY, ESQ., 650.991.5126, FOR DEFENDANTS CALTHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES; No other appearances.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES' and MARINELLA GONCALVES' Anti-Slappback Motion For An Order Dismissing This Matter In Its Entirety/Alt Dismissing This Matter IS Denied. Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's SLAPPback complaint is untimely under CCP 425.18(d)(1)(A) and (B). Further Defendants' have not shown that extraordinary circumstances exist under CCP 426.18(d)(1)(C). The Complaint filed January 8, 2013. Moving parties filed a demurrer and motion to strike on February 22, 2013. A prior motion to dismiss was denied on August 20, 2013. This motion was filed November 12, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES and MARINELLA GONCALVES' Motion To Dismiss is Denied without prejudice of refiling motion. Dismissal is not warranted. Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves move to dismiss the Complaint based on a Court order, filed on March 11, 2013, where the Court granted Defendants Milano and Littler Mendelson, PC's motion to strike SLAPPback Complaint per CCP 425.18. However, Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves were not the moving party on the motion to strike SLAPPback Complaint. The dismissal of the Complaint against Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves based on the Court Order granting Milano and Littler Mendelson, PC's motion is not warranted. To the extent that Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves argue the merits of their own anti-SLAPPback motion in the instant motion to dismiss, Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves did not state these grounds for relief in their Notice of Motion. The court cannot grant different relief, or relief on different grounds, than stated in the notice of motion. (Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 CA4th 1119, 1124, 132 CR2d 680, 684.) Moving party to prepare form of order. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN P. MORIARTY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, TEL. NO. 391-1113 X 15 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IN THE COMPLAINT is Denied without prejudice within 10 days to state proper grounds. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground to strike a prayer for relief in the form of punitive damages. (CCP 436. See Notice, p. 2:22 - 3:16.) The Court does not consider the other grounds asserted in Defendants' Points and Authorities that are not stated in the Notice. (Points & Auth., p. 10:22 - 11:15.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
